item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is damn eerie. It's still a really good piece and holds up well today. What I think works especially well is how malicious it is, even if at the end of the day it's still a regular slide. It's a nice contrast; you aren't expecting it to go down as it does when you read, and that's before the other shoe drops.

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image has not been released under a Creative Commons license and is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0. As such, it has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As someone with claustrophobia, this one really did a number on me. A very underrated skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just wanted to say that I read this years ago and that this is one of my all-time favourite articles. The idea of a bunch of children (and a couple of D-Class) trapped in some sort of time-looping claustrophobic tunnel pocket dimension forever just for being in the wrong playground at the wrong time is pure nightmare fuel. It also begs the extremely troubling question of who or what created this horror and placed it in a playground in the first place. Definitely an often overlooked classic.

+1
(Just because I can't give +2)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed it as it is, but somehow I think they'd send in at least one more D-class, possibly with a more sensitive radio. Just to be sure. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my god, my chest is tight…

I don't have physiological reactions to very many SCPs, but this one… Wow. Just wow.

Well done, you've nailed… something. Something that has reached in and is squeezing my chest and I can't breathe right! Amazing. Unpleasant, for sure, but I am thoroughly impressed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Anyone coming from the previously mentioned YT video should know there is no time loop involved in this SCP.

item.title: Re: Learned of this SCP through ForlornFoundry.
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the heads up, I'll have to check that out.

item.title: Learned of this SCP through ForlornFoundry.
item.content:encoded: YouTuber "Forlorn Foundry" did a good job making a video recreation of what goes on in the Audio Logs. This is a fantastic, FANTASTIC SCP right here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*deep breath*

AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA

but seriously, this SCP is fucking terrifying and i'm giving it a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh god, this is pretty much one of my childhood fears in SCP form. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"The object's anomalous effects only manifest when a person slides down head-first on their stomach with their arms tucked down at their sides; any other orientation of the body or limbs while sliding results in no effect, and only human beings are affected."

Upvoted; I got a good laugh out of picturing researchers performing this test with many other animals, trying to get them into that same body orientation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, excellent invocation of claustrophobia, and the repetitions and the implications they gave were chilling.

item.title: Re: cross test with 682?
item.content:encoded: no

item.title: Re: cross test with 682?
item.content:encoded: Although it is not what the author intended, I think that's an interesting idea.

item.title: One of the creepiest SCPs I've ever read
item.content:encoded:
Especially this late/early. Sheesh. Upvoted with great intensity.
I am headcanoning that they eventually get rescued for my own sanity, since I'll be going to bed very soon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so horrible. It's a perfect match for the pool noodle that teleports you to the middle of a vast empty ocean. Yuck yuck yuck never sliding or swimming again.

Also, I was so sure that when that second D-Class tried to back out he was going to bump his foot into the head of somebody trapped behind him. Either someone who will be sent in after him, or (if he really is the last person to go down the slide) the first child ever to get trapped there.

item.title: Re: cross test with 682?
item.content:encoded: no
As a child i was trapped in a small cave near my home, this took me back there. Amazing job i loved the logs.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Yeah, let's table this for now, and it will motivate me to fix up my rough draft and get it posted. I'll feel much better about linking then.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: We can totally veto this proposal and allow you to add the link once you feel comfortable with it being included. We are nothing if not flexible on the WikiWalk team.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Eugh. I don't know how I feel about linking to a series I couldn't finish, though I did finally start working on it again. I'd feel better about it if I finished and posted my current draft that is specifically about 1562.

item.title: cross test with 682?
item.content:encoded: Even if this would not kill 682, think of the one difference between the D class we have sent down there and 682. 682 would not retreat in to the mysterious stale air. I think that the slide wormhole is one way and touching it again will freeze you in time. 682 would clear out the tunnel :D

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Author has vetoed the following proposal. No edits made.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

SCP-1562 plays a major role in trennerdios' series titled Broken Light, where it is among several other related skips. Therefore, a link to that hub not only leads readers to several tales, which can always use more attention, but also to the three other SCPs linked to from that hub. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-1562 is a metal playground slide measuring 2.2 meters high and 3.4 meters long. SCP-1562 was acquired from an abandoned playground on the outskirts of ██████████, ███████ after several children in the area went missing. The object's anomalous effects only manifest when a person slides down head-first on their stomach with their arms tucked down at their sides; any other orientation of the body or limbs while sliding results in no effect, and only human beings are affected.

To this:

SCP-1562 is a metal playground slide measuring 2.2 meters high and 3.4 meters long. SCP-1562 was acquired from an abandoned playground on the outskirts of ██████████, ███████ after several children in the area went missing. The object's anomalous effects only manifest when a person slides down head-first on their stomach with their arms tucked down at their sides; any other orientation of the body or limbs while sliding results in no effect, and only human beings are affected.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent work on the logs, really. One of the creepier articles I've read in a while. Easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Amigara 2: Loop Harder (fun for kids!)

Seriously, it's everything scary about the Enigma and added in time loop tomfoolery that just makes everything worse for everyone involved

item.title: One of my favorites
item.content:encoded:
Read it a few times, always enjoy +1

The only thing I would suggest is: what if radio communication would only be severed precisely at the moment someone new enters the hole?
That would give it an air of instantaneous removal of the previous victim from our universe/killing it and leaving only a looped version of its last moments of life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the concept a lot. The ending was a little unsatisfactory for me though, just cutting off like that, but this article's got a lot of fridge horror going for it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: thats fuckin creepy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In fact, it appears the boy with the little shoes was being brave in trying to help and encourage to poor kid in front of him.

I imagine Little Shoe as being maybe 7 or 8 years old - too young to hang with the "tough" middle school crowd and instead one of the older ones watching out for the little kids on the swingsets and slides. A young friend or sibling of Little Shoe disappears from the slide, perhaps with a muffled yelp, and Little Shoe sets off to help with the sort of determined face that can only be mustered by a 7-year-old with no knowledge of the horror in the world and no reason to imagine what he will soon face.

Yea, this is one of the first scips in a long time that terrified me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooohhh… when I read the "loop" tag, I assumed that the little boy was one of the Class-D's in the past… or something. I guess I'm overthinking things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy flippin' cannoli, this is one of those rare SCP's that actually turned my blood to ice water for a moment. Here's your +1…now I'm going to crawl in a corner and shiver for a while…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep, this. And he's not the first child to get stuck in there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dammit. Very creepy.

If radio still works, maybe a rough position could be triangulated?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An unfortuante kid that went down the slide, stuck in the slide's loop, I think.

Looking back on this article, I suddenly appreciate it tenfold. Much creepier when I think about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get it. +1'ed for how unsettling it is, but I feel that I am missing something. Who's the little boy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really liked this. It's plenty bizarre and creepy on its own but gives material for a lot of speculations. I think this has potential for great Foundation Tales too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1,I like this creepy hopelessness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember reading the draft a couple weeks ago, and I was hoping you posted it. Sure enough, while browsing through Series 2 to count the remaining slots, I found it!

+1! This is really good, and I'm glad you added it to the main list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Playgrounds are frickin' scary, am I right?

This is cool for the way it kept me thinking. What if you threw lots of stuff in there? Would it eventually fill up? I assume each new layer isn't aware of the layers that arrive after it…

I'm a little surprised they didn't chuck any stuff down it like GPS receivers, but hey, it works perfectly well as-is.

Happy to +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That appears to be an all metal slide, in a desert, with no shade in sight; I think I got butt-burnt just looking at it.
That thing was a menace long before temporal entombment came into play.

While I usually prefer gestalt, all-at-the-same-time style time travel mechanics; this is probably the creepiest use of the iterational, layer-after-layer style I've ever read. Kudos.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First geniunely creepy thing I've read in a while that's not a 2000 submission. Nice job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The thought of being caged inside a small tunnel forever makes this creepy.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love reading this after seeing it on Reddit. I like the changes you made. You added another depth of creepiness to the already scary idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
FYI I finally read that. It was a pretty interesting idea, and I see why you were reminded of it.

Then I found this, and it sorta lost the creepy factor:

DDR

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I agree. Changed it back, because I really do think that works better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't see what the problem with object was. Changing it to subject actually makes the tone worse. Playground slide is not a subject; playground slide isn't alive. Referring to it as subject was the only tone issue I noticed while reading this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a perfect example of why I love this website. +1 so much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…yeesh.

Pure creepy isn't usually my thing, but this works for me. I feel compelled to note that it would be boring as fuck if the logs weren't there, but they are and it's not.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: THIS GIVES ME SHIVERS. I'm claustrophobic, but even putting that aside this is so creepy. Well done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey thanks! Reddit gave me some great feedback that I took to heart. The article wouldn't be in its current form if I hadn't gone that route for some of my feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The day I saw this one on reddit, it was just REALLY good. Now with the changes you made, it's even better! I'm so glad it exists. The idea is great and the logs just creeped me out. You've totally got my vote ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Accidental Double-Post. Ignore

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God Fucking Dammit
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, it's just a coincidence that 1932 was posted right before mine. And the playground element really doesn't play much of a part in my SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really well-written, but I feel like there are way too many playground-themed skips. Still, tentative upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A creepy mix of Amigara Fault, Ted the Caver, the Red Dwarf Future Echoes episode, and that horrible bit in Watership Down where the warren is filled in…

*dips his +1 in liquid gold before passing it to you*

Bravo!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hm. I think some elements are a touch familiar… but, hell, I liked it, and it's pretty damn well done. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This…this creeped me out. A lot. Like whoa. The last log was f'in creepy. I never thought I'd be scared by a slide. Good job. I'm now forever avoiding playgrounds.

…take my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1. This feels like a classic" scip, with a simple concept excellently told.
